market_context: Period opened the Tuesday after Presidents Day (Feb 16 NYSE holiday). Macro backdrop: US economic growth slowed to 1.4% Q4 2025 (from 4.4% Q3), consumer sentiment weak (UMich 57.3), tariff uncertainty escalating with Trump proposing global tariff hikes. The AI capex narrative bifurcated sharply — hyperscalers (Meta, Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft) collectively guided $470B+ in 2026 capex, but markets punished the spend rather than rewarded it. The headline event was NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings reported Feb 25 after close: revenue $68.1B (+73% YoY), net income +94%, Q1 FY27 guidance $78B — all beats — yet stock fell ~5% the next day (classic buy-the-rumor sell-the-news). Feb 24 produced the AMD-Meta 6GW GPU deal ($60B estimated, MI450 architecture), sending AMD +8.8% and creating a clear divergence: NVDA priced for perfection, AMD repricing from a lower base. PLTR benefited from Trump defense spending surge and DOGE government software contracts. GLD was climbing on tariff/geopolitical risk but reversed sharply mid-March as risk-off trade unwound. The week of Feb 23-27 ended with hot PPI data (Feb 27) and broad market selloff — Nasdaq down >3% for February, its worst month since March 2025. The Magnificent Seven index fell 7.3% in February overall, while equal-weight S&P +3.5% showed rotation away from mega-cap growth.

trades.thesis: First trading day after Presidents Day. PLTR riding Trump defense/DOGE wave: $10B Army contract, Maven Smart System expansion, $900M+ new federal contracts pipeline. Government AI software is structural spend immune to DeepSeek disruption. Stock had mild pullback from highs, offered re-entry. Defense AI is the one AI vertical where capex scrutiny doesn't apply — it's mandated policy spend.
trades.catalyst: Trump defense budget increase to $1.5T, DOGE federal software modernization contracts, Army multiyear AI platform deal announced Jan 2026
trades.lesson: Government AI spending (PLTR) decoupled from private sector AI capex scrutiny in this period. When the investment thesis is policy-mandated rather than ROI-dependent, it survives the 'will AI pay off?' narrative. Conviction justified; 20-day hold captured the full move to target.

trades.thesis: Tariff escalation risk accelerating — Trump proposing additional global tariff hikes on top of 20% China tariffs. Safe-haven bid for gold should persist. GLD had been in strong uptrend Feb 14-18, reflecting flight from risk assets. Macro backdrop of slowing GDP (1.4% Q4) + tariff uncertainty = classic gold setup. Position sizing modest as this is a hedge, not a high-conviction alpha trade.
trades.catalyst: Trump tariff escalation announcements, slowing Q4 GDP (1.4%), geopolitical uncertainty
trades.lesson: GLD did rally to ~$490 (Mar 2) before reversing hard. The 20-day exit (Mar 18) caught the reversal. Gold was a short-term safe haven trade, not a 20-day hold — should have set tighter exit or taken profit near $490. Lesson: safe-haven trades require momentum exits, not fixed holding periods.

trades.thesis: AMD trading at significant discount to NVDA on AI GPU narrative. DeepSeek fears have faded — the efficiency paradox (Jevons: cheaper AI inference = more total compute demand) is re-establishing. AMD MI300X gaining enterprise adoption, and rumors of hyperscaler partnerships (Meta deal not yet public). Pre-NVDA earnings positioning: if NVDA beats, AMD benefits from rising AI capex tide. Lower valuation provides cushion.
trades.catalyst: AI capex cycle resumption, AMD MI300X enterprise adoption, relative value vs NVDA, pre-NVDA earnings positioning
trades.lesson: Flat win — AMD did rally hard on Feb 24 (Meta deal +8.8%) but then gave it back over March. The 20-day holding period missed the optimal exit. Entry thesis was correct but timing required active management. AMD's Feb 24 catalyst should have been the exit trigger, not the 20-day mechanical rule.

trades.thesis: NVDA reporting Q4 FY2026 earnings ~Feb 25. Blackwell GPU ramp in H2 FY2026 should be confirmed. Street expecting $65.9B revenue — prior quarters have seen massive beats. Positioning into earnings with 5-day runway. High-conviction AI infrastructure demand from all hyperscalers ($470B+ capex 2026). Risk: sell-the-news reaction. Sized accordingly — entered 5 days pre-earnings.
trades.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings expected ~Feb 25, Blackwell GPU ramp confirmation, hyperscaler capex guidance
trades.lesson: Classic earnings trap: NVDA reported blowout numbers (revenue $68.1B, +73% YoY, net income +94%) but stock fell 5% on Feb 26 and continued deteriorating through mid-March (stop loss at $178 should have been honored). H1 Lesson L4 explicitly warns: NVDA earnings creates sell-the-news; should have PASSED or gone SHORT. The 20-day exit captured further losses. Fundamental rule violation.

trades.thesis: Meta tumbled >8% post-Q4 earnings on AI capex blowout concerns despite strong revenue. $470B+ combined hyperscaler capex for 2026 is indigestible for market — Zuckerberg signaling massive ROI timeline. META now trading on 2029 AI monetization — too much risk. Market jittery on Monday (Dow -821 pts). DeepSeek disruption narrative still present. Short the capex fear story: investors will punish capex-heavy names for months.
trades.catalyst: Post-earnings capex concern overhang, META tumbled >8% week prior, Monday market selloff (-1.13% Nasdaq)
trades.lesson: Correct call — capex fear overhang persisted and META continued lower through March (hit $525 by Mar 27). The 20-day exit at $604 underperformed the full move. Short capex-blowout names in the window between earnings and next catalyst is a reliable pattern when market sentiment is fragile.

trades.thesis: AMD-Meta 6GW GPU deal announced today: Meta committing to 6 gigawatts of AMD Instinct GPUs (MI450 architecture), estimated $60B over 5 years. AMD jumped 8.8% intraday. This is a structural shift — AMD breaks NVDA's AI monopoly with a hyperscaler anchor customer. Momentum trade into close after confirming the deal. Risk: some sell-the-news after initial pop, but 20-day horizon should capture sustained re-rating.
trades.catalyst: AMD-Meta 6GW Instinct GPU partnership announced Feb 24 2026, estimated $60B contract, first 1GW deployment H2 2026
trades.lesson: Bought the news catalyst — AMD rallied 8.8% the day the deal was announced, but the entry was at the day's close after the pop. Classic buy-the-catalyst at the top of the initial move. Should have waited 2-3 days for the initial excitement to fade before entering. The 20-day exit saw AMD give back the pop. Rule: for binary catalyst events, enter 2 days after announcement, not on announcement day.

trades.thesis: NVDA reporting Q4 FY2026 after close today. H1 Lesson L4: NVDA earnings creates sell-the-news reaction — stock already up 5% from Feb 17 lows heading into report. Street has priced in perfection ($65.9B consensus vs likely $68B beat). Even on a massive beat, the stock tends to fade post-earnings as the 'what's next' concern dominates. High-valuation AI leader, tariff risk on TSMC supply chain. Short into earnings with thesis that reaction will be negative within 20 days even on a beat.
trades.catalyst: NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings after close, sell-the-news setup, H1 L4 lesson, tariff/supply chain overhang, OpenAI deal uncertainty
trades.lesson: H1 L4 lesson directly confirmed: NVDA reported $68.1B revenue (+73% YoY), EPS beat, $78B Q1 guidance — all blowout — yet stock fell 5% next day (Feb 26) and continued declining through March. Short into earnings on NVDA is the right structural trade when stock is near highs and priced for perfection. The lesson is validated.

trades.thesis: NVDA down 5% post-earnings dragging Nasdaq lower. Broader tech weakness from: (1) AI capex scrutiny hitting multiple names, (2) tariff uncertainty, (3) hot PPI data signaling sticky inflation, (4) Magnificent Seven index down 7.3% for February. QQQ provides index-level short with diversified risk. February was the worst Nasdaq month since March 2025. Positioning for continued weakness through late March.
trades.catalyst: NVDA post-earnings selloff spreading to Nasdaq, hot PPI data, AI capex fear, tariff escalation, Nasdaq -3% month
trades.lesson: Index short worked cleanly. When multiple headwinds converge (AI capex fear + tariffs + hot inflation + earnings disappointment), shorting QQQ is cleaner than picking individual names — captures sector weakness without single-stock idiosyncratic risk. QQQ fell from $608 to $573 (-5.7%) over the 20-day window.

trades.thesis: Hot PPI data on Feb 27 (last trading day of month) triggers broad selloff. TSLA faces triple headwinds: (1) Musk fully distracted by DOGE/government role, reducing Tesla operational focus; (2) brand damage from political polarization reducing EV demand in key markets; (3) slowing consumer spending (Q4 consumption growth 2.4% vs 3.5% prior) hurts discretionary EV purchases. Stock elevated vs fundamentals. Week-end tax-loss selling adds pressure.
trades.catalyst: Hot PPI data (Feb 27), Musk DOGE distraction, brand damage, consumer spending slowdown, month-end selling pressure
trades.lesson: TSLA short worked as multiple headwinds compounded — Musk brand damage + consumer slowdown + broader market weakness. TSLA fell from $402 to $361 (-10%) over 20 days. Key insight: when a high-profile CEO becomes a political liability and consumer sentiment is weakening, the demand destruction is real and measurable. Musk's political role reduced willingness-to-pay among key Tesla demographic.

period_summary.regime_notes: Feb 17-28 2026 was a regime inflection point: AI capex narrative shifted from 'spend = bull signal' to 'spend = ROI question mark.' NVDA earnings blowout triggered a sell-the-news that cascaded through QQQ and high-valuation tech. Three clear regimes within the period: (1) Pre-earnings cautious optimism (Feb 17-24), (2) NVDA earnings catalyst + AMD deal (Feb 24-25), (3) Post-earnings broad tech selloff + hot PPI (Feb 26-27). Winning trades concentrated in: government AI (PLTR), NVDA short (earnings rule L4), index short (QQQ), consumer sentiment short (TSLA). Losing trades: pre-earnings NVDA long (rule violation), post-catalyst AMD entry (bought the top), GLD (good direction, wrong exit timing). Key learning: Feb 2026 marked the peak of the AI narrative honeymoon — from here, the market demanded proof of ROI, not just capex commitments.
